response.justification: Szabo demonstrates strong technical breadth across microservices, high-throughput systems, and recent exposure to agentic AI tooling at Google. However, he falls significantly short of core CTO requirements: no demonstrated track record scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people or developing senior technical leadership layers, minimal fintech infrastructure experience, limited evidence of operating production multi-agent AI systems (Google role appears focused on tooling rather than production systems), and no background operating in regulated financial environments. His background is predominantly individual-contributor focused rather than organizational leadership.
strengths: Strong technical foundation with 17 years of engineering experience including microservices, high-throughput systems (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby), and event-driven architecture
Recent hands-on exposure to agentic AI frameworks and MCP tooling at Google, demonstrating ability to learn emerging technologies quickly
Proven software craftsmanship emphasis with TDD, testing rigor, and knowledge-sharing through workshops and mentoring across multiple organizations
concerns: No demonstrated experience scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people or building senior technical leadership layers—all roles appear to be individual contributors or small team contributors
Limited production experience with multi-agent AI systems; Google role appears focused on tooling and frameworks rather than operating stateful AI systems in financial decision-making contexts
Minimal fintech infrastructure expertise and no evidence of operating systems in regulated financial environments with compliance, audit, or credit risk requirements

key_factors.factor: Production multi-agent AI operations in fintech
Technical foundation and system design capability
